
# Exercise 4.2.2

```{=typst}


#set math.equation(numbering: n => [(4.2.2.#n)], supplement: none)

Build the matrix $B$ for a three-node bar element with nodal coordinates $(x_1, y_1)$, $(x_2, y_2)$, and $(x_3, y_3)$.

== Solution

We can estimate the displacement with the use of shape functions:

$
  bold(u)(x,y,z) approx bold(N)(x,y,z)bold(U)^((e))
$

Where $bold(U)^((e))$ is a collection of the displacement degrees of freedom.

Analysing a single node in its x displacement, it is true that

$
  u_x(x,y,z) = sum_(i=1)^n N_(i(x,y,z)) u_(i x) 
$

Deriving in relation to x:

$
  epsilon_(x x) = (partial u_x) / (partial x) = sum_(i=1)^(n) (partial N_i) / (partial x) u_(i x)
$

Thus, the $bold(B)$ matrix represents an element to generalize the relation between the strain vector and displacement matrix:

$
  bmat(
    epsilon_(x x);
    epsilon_(y y);
    gamma_(x y);
  )
  =
  bold(B) dot
  bmat(
    u_(1x);
    u_(1y);
    u_(2x);
    u_(2y);
    dots.v;
    u_(n x);
    u_(n y)
  )
$ 

which is simply $bold(epsilon) = bold(B) bold(U)^((e))$. $bold(B)$ on itself can be represented as a union of matrixes:

$
  bold(B)
  =
  mat(bold(B)_1, bold(B)_2, bold(B)_3, bold(B)_4, bold(B)_5, dots.h, bold(B)_n)
$

Since our element can only suffer strain from compression/traction - that is, we assume it to be that of a truss, our equilibrium will be

 $ epsilon_(s s) = bold(B) bold(U)^((e)) = bmat(bold(B)_1, bold(B)_2, bold(B)_3) bmat(u_(1x); u_(1y); u_(2x); u_(2y); u_(3x); u_(3y)) $

Where

$
  bold(B)_i = mat((partial N_i) / (partial x), (partial N_i) / (partial y))
$

To define $bold(B)_i$, we can start from the strain definition

$
  epsilon_(s s) = (partial u_s) / (partial s)
$

We know that, via the chain rule

$
  (partial N_i) / (partial s) = (d N_i) / (d xi) dot (d xi) / (d s)
$ <partial>

$
  J = ||bold(J)|| = ||mat((d x) / (d xi), (d y) / (d xi))^T|| = sqrt(((d x) / (d xi))^2+((d y) / (d xi))^2) = (d s) / (d xi)
$
Hence
$
  1 / J = (d xi) / (d s)
$ <subto>

Substituting #ref(<subto>) into #ref(<partial>)  we get

$
  (partial N_i) / (partial s) = 1 / J (d N_i) / (d xi) 
$
$
  bold(B)_i = mat((partial N_i) / (partial x), (partial N_i) / (partial y))
  =
  mat(
    (partial N_i) / (partial s) (partial x) / (partial xi) (partial xi) / (partial s),
    (partial N_i) / (partial s) (partial y) / (partial xi) (partial xi) / (partial s),
  )
  =
  (partial N_i) / (partial s) (partial xi) / (partial s) mat((partial x) / (partial xi), (partial y) / (partial xi))

$

Which finally equates to

$
  bold(B)_i = 1 / (J^2) (d N_i) / (d xi) mat((partial x) / (partial xi), (partial y) / (partial xi))
$



```

# Exercise 4.2.3

```{=typst}

#set math.equation(numbering: n => [(4.2.3.#n)], supplement: none)

Find the matrix $B$ for a three-node triangular element with nodal coordinates $(x_1, y_1)$, $(x_2, y_2)$, and $(x_3, y_3)$. Why is matrix $B$ constant along the element, and what does that imply?

== Solution
solution text

```

# Exercise 4.2.4

```{=typst}

#set math.equation(numbering: n => [(4.2.4.#n)], supplement: none)

For the quadrilateral element shown below, and given its nodal coordinates and nodal-displacement vector

$ C = mat(0, 0; 4, 2; 4, 4; 0, 2) "m" quad "and" quad U^((e)) = mat(0; 0; 0.01; 0.01; 0.015; 0.015; 0; 0.015) "m" $

as shown in the figure. Approximate the strain vector at the natural coordinates $(xi, eta) = (-sqrt(1/3), sqrt(1/3))$.

== Solution
solution text

```

# Exercise 4.2.5

```{=typst}

#set math.equation(numbering: n => [(4.2.5.#n)], supplement: none)

Find an expression for the matrix $B$ of a three-node bar element in 2D as a function of the Jacobian and the local coordinate $xi$. Consider $epsilon = (partial u) / (partial s)$, where $s$ is the curvilinear coordinate along the bar path.

== Solution
solution text

```
